pattern: next/font with Inter as SF Pro fallback
implementation: import { Inter } from 'next/font/google'; const inter = Inter({ subsets: ['latin'] display: 'swap' }); export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return <html className={inter.className}> }
anti_pattern: Loading Google Fonts via <link> tag or @import in CSS
notes: next/font eliminates layout shift and self-hosts font for performance matching Apple's load speed

pattern: App Router layout.tsx for consistent chrome
implementation: export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return <html><body><NavBar />{children}<Footer /></body></html> }
anti_pattern: Pages Router _app.tsx with manual layout wrapping
notes: App Router layouts persist across navigation without remounting preserving Apple nav feel

pattern: Server Components for fast initial load
implementation:
async function ProductList() { const products = await fetchProducts(); return
    {products.map(p => )}
}
anti_pattern: Client components fetching data on mount with useEffect
notes: Zero JS shipped for data-fetching components matches Apple.com's performance profile

implementation: <Image src='/hero.jpg' alt='Hero' width={1200} height={600} priority placeholder='blur' blurDataURL={blurUrl} />
anti_pattern: tag without optimization or priority hint
notes: Eliminates LCP bottleneck; blur placeholder matches Apple's progressive image loading

implementation: export const metadata: Metadata = { title: 'Product – Brand' description: 'Description' openGraph: { images: [{ url: '/og.jpg' width: 1200 height: 630 }] } viewport: { width: 'device-width' initialScale: 1 viewportFit: 'cover' } }
anti_pattern: <Head> with manual meta tags in each page
notes: Single metadata export covers OG image and viewport for PWA and sharing

implementation: app/(marketing)/page.tsx uses MarketingLayout; app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx uses AppLayout
anti_pattern: Single layout wrapping all routes with conditional rendering
notes: Separate layout trees for marketing and authenticated app without URL impact

implementation:
export default function Loading() { return
}
anti_pattern: Spinner-only loading states or no loading UI
notes: Apple apps show content silhouettes at correct size to prevent layout shift on load

pattern: error.tsx with clean Apple error design
implementation:
'use client'; export default function Error({ reset }: { reset: () => void }) { return

Something went wrong

}
anti_pattern: Generic browser error page or noisy stack traces exposed to users
notes: Clean minimal error pages match Apple's design principle of calm technology

implementation: app/@modal/(.)photos/[id]/page.tsx intercepts /photos/[id] to show as overlay; app/layout.tsx renders {modal} slot alongside {children}
anti_pattern: Client-side state for modal open/close with URL not reflecting modal state
notes: URL-addressable modals match iOS sheet behavior; deep-linkable and browser-history-aware

implementation: export async function generateStaticParams() { const products = await getProducts(); return products.map(p => ({ slug: p.slug })) }
anti_pattern: getServerSideProps for pages that rarely change
notes: Pre-rendered pages served from CDN edge match Apple.com's near-instant page loads

implementation: const HeavyChart = dynamic(() => import('@/components/HeavyChart') { loading: () => <ChartSkeleton /> ssr: false })
anti_pattern: Importing all components eagerly in page files
notes: Reduces initial JS payload keeping time-to-interactive fast for Apple-caliber performance

pattern: CSS custom properties for Apple color tokens
implementation: :root { --color-text-primary: #1d1d1f; --color-text-secondary: #86868b; --color-bg: #fbfbfd; --color-blue: #2997ff; --color-separator: rgba(0 0 0 / 0.15); } @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { :root { --color-bg: #000000; --color-text-primary: #f5f5f7; } }
anti_pattern: Hardcoded hex values in component CSS or Tailwind arbitrary values
notes: Centralized semantic color tokens flip automatically with system dark mode

implementation: nav { backdrop-filter: saturate(180%) blur(20px); background: rgba(255 255 255 / 0.72); border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(0 0 0 / 0.1); position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 100; }
anti_pattern: Opaque solid background on navigation bar
notes: Apple's signature frosted glass nav replicates material translucency on the web

implementation: const observer = new IntersectionObserver(entries => entries.forEach(e => e.isIntersecting && e.target.classList.add('visible')) { threshold: 0.1 }); useEffect(() => { observer.observe(ref.current); return () => observer.disconnect() } [])
anti_pattern: scroll event listener for reveal animations
notes: Performant off-main-thread intersection detection matches Apple.com fade-in sections

implementation: import { ThemeProvider } from 'next-themes'; <ThemeProvider attribute='class' defaultTheme='system' enableSystem>; CSS: [data-theme='dark'] { --color-bg: #000000; }
anti_pattern: JavaScript-only dark mode toggle ignoring system preference
notes: System-level dark mode preference respected without flash of wrong theme on load

pattern: CSS env() for safe areas on mobile web
implementation: body { padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom); } .tab-bar { padding-bottom: calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom) + 8px); }
anti_pattern: Hardcoded bottom padding for iPhone home indicator clearance
notes: Adapts to home indicator and Dynamic Island on all iPhone models in Safari

pattern: Apple touch icon and manifest for PWA
implementation: <link rel='apple-touch-icon' sizes='180x180' href='/apple-touch-icon.png'>; manifest.json: { 'display': 'standalone' 'theme_color': '#fbfbfd' 'background_color': '#ffffff' }
anti_pattern: Generic favicon only without Apple touch icon or web app manifest
notes: Enables Add to Home Screen with correct icon and splash colors matching brand

implementation: import { motion } from 'framer-motion'; <motion.div initial={{ opacity: 0 y: 20 }} animate={{ opacity: 1 y: 0 }} transition={{ type: 'spring' stiffness: 300 damping: 30 }}>
anti_pattern: CSS ease-in-out transitions for interactive elements like cards and modals
notes: Spring physics match UIKit's default animation model for natural motion

implementation: <section className='max-w-[980px] mx-auto px-5'>{children}</section>
anti_pattern: Full viewport width text blocks or inconsistent container widths
notes: Apple.com constrains reading line length to 980px for comfortable typography

implementation: <section className='py-[80px] md:py-[120px]'> or "padding-block: clamp(80px 10vw 120px)"
anti_pattern: Tight sections with 24px padding or inconsistent vertical spacing
notes: Apple uses generous breathing room between content sections as a core design language element

pattern: Large hero text matching Apple style
implementation: Next-generation performance.
anti_pattern: Standard paragraph-sized or medium hero headings
notes: Oversized bold headlines with tight tracking are Apple's primary brand expression

pattern: WebP responsive images with blur placeholder
implementation: <Image src={src} alt={alt} sizes='(max-width: 768px) 100vw 50vw' placeholder='blur' blurDataURL={blurDataUrl} />
anti_pattern: Static src without srcset or format optimization
notes: next/image handles WebP conversion srcset generation and lazy loading automatically

implementation: font-feature-settings: 'kern' 1 'liga' 1 'calt' 1; -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale;
anti_pattern: Default browser font rendering on retina displays
notes: Enables ligatures kerning and antialiasing for crisp SF Pro-equivalent rendering

pattern: CSS container queries for responsive components
implementation: @container (min-width: 480px) { .card { flex-direction: row; } }; .card-wrapper { container-type: inline-size; }
anti_pattern: Media queries that respond to viewport not component context
notes: Container-aware components match the flexibility of SwiftUI's adaptive layout modifiers

implementation: letter-spacing: -0.003em on 32px text; letter-spacing: -0.005em on 64px+ text
anti_pattern: Default browser letter-spacing on large headings
notes: Tight tracking on large text is a defining Apple typographic characteristic
